representative_scenario: After release of the radiology report or discharge summary; parties dispute whether the patient-facing conclusion matches what was originally anchored. Post-hoc audit checks whether the presented output still matches the originally committed output-side evidence.
how_we_simulate_post_hoc_audit: Inject output-side tamper into off-chain canonical_payload for a chosen fraction of requests; run full commit–anchor–fetch–audit loop; aggregate whether tampered rows are flagged and compare to benign baseline.
tamper_mutation_in_code: Mutates output_summary.output_label (e.g. appends tamper tag). See audit_pipeline._tamper_output_label.
audit_verification_mechanism: Verifier reconstructs committed scope from off-chain artifacts and compares to commitments; pipeline writes tamper_audit.csv rows and aggregates detection in experiment_summary.
expected_result: The system should support traceable audit by checking consistency between the current record and the anchored output-side summary or label; quantify with detection rate and benign behavior.
primary_commands: run_med_csv_integration.py --tamper-strategy output --tamper-rate 0.1 (or run_attack_detection_matrix.py including output row)
output_artifacts: outputs/records/<tag>/tamper_audit.csv; experiment_summary.json; attack_detection_matrix.csv/.json output row for output
methods_notes: Implementation mutates structured output_label; if the paper stresses full paragraph text align Methods with dataset fields.

representative_scenario: Dispute over whether cited guidelines or retrieved passages match what was bound at inference time (evidence swap or omission).
how_we_simulate_post_hoc_audit: Use context strategy to mutate input or context-side summary fields; same audit loop. If input_summary is missing the code may fall back to output-side mutation—disclose in the report.
tamper_mutation_in_code: Primarily mutates input_summary.modality; if missing falls back to _tamper_output_label. See _tamper_offchain_payload context branch.
audit_verification_mechanism: Same verification path; binding depends on whether input or context summaries are present in canonical_payload for your CSV schema.
expected_result: Post-hoc audit should flag detectable inconsistency on the context side when those fields are actually committed.
output_artifacts: Same as above; attack_detection_matrix.csv/.json context row or column
methods_notes: If full evidence text is not in structured summaries the threat model must say so; current mutation emphasizes modality.

representative_scenario: Claimed model card weights or inference settings disagree with the anchored record; audit checks model-side binding.
how_we_simulate_post_hoc_audit: Use model strategy to cascade tamper through model then governance then context then output; first existing block is mutated.
tamper_mutation_in_code: Cascade: _tamper_model_inference_config; if no model_summary try governance context then output. See strategy model branch.
audit_verification_mechanism: Verification compares model-bound fields; detection changes strongly with payload_ablation e.g. no_model—use ablation runs to explain binding scope.
expected_result: Support traceable check that model-related evidence matches the anchor; combine matrix model row with optional ablation.
primary_commands: run_med_csv_integration.py --tamper-strategy model; attack matrix; optional run_med_csv_integration --payload-ablation no_model
output_artifacts: experiment_summary.json; attack_detection_matrix.csv/.json; compare experiment_summary across payload_ablation runs
methods_notes: Align narrative with inference_config text mutation rather than only a version string; state clearly in Methods.

representative_scenario: Dispute whether the prompt template or institutional policy version matches the record; mapped to governance metadata vs anchor (not full prompt text unless stored).
how_we_simulate_post_hoc_audit: Use governance cascade governance then context then output; simulates inconsistency in policy_version and other governance-bound fields.
tamper_mutation_in_code: Cascade: _tamper_governance_policy_version; if no governance_summary then context then output. See strategy governance.
audit_verification_mechanism: Verifier checks governance-bound fields; under no_governance ablation detection weakens—shows the dispute is hard to audit when policy is not bound.
expected_result: When governance fields are committed the system should support traceable reconciliation; otherwise Methods must state binding limits.
primary_commands: run_med_csv_integration.py --tamper-strategy governance; matrix governance row; compare no_governance ablation
output_artifacts: tamper_audit.csv; attack_detection_matrix.csv/.json; optional ablation CSV exports
methods_notes: No dedicated prompt_text tamper branch; prefer wording policy_version or governance metadata to avoid over-claiming.

representative_scenario: Clinical disagreement over advice text versus process compliance; split mapping—altered displayed advice maps to output tamper; non-compliant run maps to model or governance.
how_we_simulate_post_hoc_audit: Reproduce by dispute type: separate rows for output model governance; or layer_quarter as unspecified multi-layer stress. Do not equate pure opinion disagreement with tamper detection.
implementation_strategy: output OR model OR governance OR layer_quarter
tamper_mutation_in_code: See rows 6.3-1 through 6.3-4; layer_quarter rotates one of four layers per row (model governance context output).
audit_verification_mechanism: Same audit machinery per strategy; keep qualitative disputes separate from synthetic tamper experiments.
expected_result: Traceable audit yields a testable consistent or inconsistent verdict for a chosen tamper type; cite matrix rows not one number for all disputes.
primary_commands: Multiple tags or run_attack_detection_matrix.py with attacks output model governance layer_quarter; map dispute type to strategy in a paper table
methods_notes: Do not claim the system resolves all clinical disagreements; only record-vs-anchor inconsistency is testable here.
